mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing the present value of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) following rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during periods of rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during an economic expansion reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain efficiency, which immediately boosts market sentiment and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative 5-day returns in over 50% of triggered cases, or predictive accuracy drops below random chance during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer price inflation combined with a stagnant federal funds rate compresses real interest rates and erodes purchasing power, forcing markets to price in aggressive future tightening and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with payroll growth trends.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP growth below 1.5 percent signals demand contraction and recession risk, which immediately depresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The market produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict downturns with a t-statistic greater than 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment confirms productive capacity utilization and labor market tightening, which creates immediate bullish momentum and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal generates negative 5-day returns in over 50% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive significance (p > 0.10) in walk-forward validation.
